home.noMedicinesHint: Add your first medicine in Settings to start logging.

warning.nextOk: This dose should be OK from {time}.

safety.overIntakeHard: {amount} is well above your max per intake ({max}).
safety.overIntakeSoft: {amount} is above your max per intake ({max}).
safety.overDaily: This would exceed your daily max ({max}) — {used} already taken in the last 24 h.
safety.nearDaily: You are close to your daily max ({max}) — {used} taken in the last 24 h.
safety.tooSoonHard: Much less than your minimum interval of {minutes} min since the last dose.
safety.tooSoonSoft: Less than your minimum interval of {minutes} min since the last dose.
safety.tooSoonLinear: It's a bit early since your last dose — some of it is still working.

settings.title: Settings
settings.name: Name
settings.presetsHint: These become the big buttons on the home screen.
settings.minIntervalHint: If your doctor said "at least X hours apart", set it here.
settings.archiveConfirm: Archive this medicine? Its history is kept.
settings.wakeWindowHint: Your daily max is spread over these hours when computing dose spacing.
settings.countUncertainHint: Safest is on: an unsure dose is treated as taken.

help.title: Help
help.gettingStartedBody: Add your medicines under Settings → Medicines, with the doses you normally take and (optionally) your max per intake and per day. Then log a dose with one tap on the home screen.
help.colorsYellow: Yellow: a bit early or close to your daily max — you'll be asked to confirm.
help.colorsRed: Red: this would exceed your max per intake or per day. You can still log it (reality wins), but only after an explicit confirmation.
help.spacingBody: If your max is, say, 3 mg per day spread over your waking hours, a 1 mg dose "occupies" about a third of the day. The app allows a little slack, so slightly early — or smaller doses more often — stays green. Adjust strictness and waking hours in Settings.
help.unsureBody: That's the point of this app: open it and look at the top of each card. If you truly don't remember, log with "Not sure I took it" — it shows up faded in history and (by default) counts in the safety math, which is the safe assumption.
help.backdateBody: Use the ⋯ button on a medicine card to log a dose that happened earlier, change the amount, or add a note.
help.installBody: On iPhone: open in Safari → Share → "Add to Home Screen". On Android/Chrome: menu → "Install app". On desktop: the install icon in the address bar.
help.disclaimer: This app is a logging aid, not medical advice. Always follow your prescription and your doctor's instructions.

onboarding.welcomeBody: Never wonder "did I take it?" again. Log every dose with one tap, and the app keeps track for you.
onboarding.step1Body: Set up each medicine with your usual doses and optional daily limits.
onboarding.step2Body: One tap logs a dose now. The ⋯ button handles other amounts, earlier times, or "not sure".
onboarding.step3Body: Buttons turn yellow or red if you're about to take too much too soon.
onboarding.disclaimerAccept: I understand this is not medical advice
